author: potuz
content: So I don't think the proposer lookahead can change if the switch to compounding issue is fixed though.

author: nflaig
content: from my analysis I did so far that seems true but <@688748669268132001> should probably chime in

author: nflaig
content: in any case, the whole construction seems really fragile to me, the fact that this edge case exists is concerning

author: potuz
content: with 4 epochs of missing blocks it does change of course

author: potuz
content: the construction of what? the GENESIS_SLOT special casing for the consolidation request? if that's the statement then I agree with you, probably this was added to help motivate people to switch to compounding

author: nflaig
content:
I mean that you have `payload_states` and `block_states` that coexist for the same block root and can diverge when running `process_epoch`

so  `dependent_root` can be unchanged but actually the state is different, the impact right now seems to only affect `proposer_lookahead` (not shuffling) but still

author: tbenr
content:
I have the same feeling. This complexity can easily be a source of bugs.
I believe, even if "it seems fine for almost all cases" we should add explicit tests to ensure all clients will use the same state

author: tbenr
content: the reason is we don't want to have edgecases that can become less edgy in future forks

author: potuz
content: dependent_root is the problem here though which is not part of consensus

author: potuz
content: it can easily be the hash or the block_root,  or root + bool

author: potuz
content: but the actual dependent root is that combination

author: potuz
content: I don't think there's any problem with having two different states that advanced to the next epoch are divergent, they are already divergent before we advance

author: potuz
content: that's the whole point of ePBS

author: potuz
content: I think the problem is not on consensus, rather on the API, for all of these "issues"

author: tbenr
content: yep. but maybe an additional check in reference tests to verify the actual computed committees, or the state hash used to compute them.. something like that

author: potuz
content: yeah we need to add a *ton* of tests with a full epoch worth of missing blocks

author: potuz
content: and this very particular case of the switch to compounding validator happening in the last payload

author: tbenr
content: it really look like a good work for fuzzers
